pages.raw_text:
The attendance at the meeting of
the trades council last night was I'm
usually large. Recently great inter
est has been evinced by the delegates
In the proceedings Of the central body
and this IL attributed to the infus-
Ion of new blood since the beginning
Of the year. The new delegates are
entering into the work of organiza-
tion with enthusiasm, evidently fol-
lowing the example of Delegate ]. C.
Daily. who has the reputation of be-
ing one Of the best organizers on the
Pacific coast.


No business Of Importance was
transacted last night. most Of the
time being devoted to hearing reports
froc committees and to seating the
delegates.


The new scale submitted by the
building trades to the council for
its approval was briefly considered
and 1t was determined that no action
be taken at present.


1t was reported that all the barber
shops in the city. with two excep-
tioiS, are thoroughly unlOT1lZeu.


The difficulty between the brewery
workers and one of the local brew
cries has been satisfactorily adjusted


The ACCESSIBILITY of forming the
printing trades into an allied body
is being discussed. and. as Spokane
is said to be the only city on the
coast which has no organization Of
this kind it Is very probable that the
allied trades will be formed. 1t will
be composed of the pressmen and
pressfeeders, printers and bookbind-
ers. Such an organization Is deemed
advisable, as 1t would foster feeling
Of harmony and fraternity among the
workers of these crafts.


The officers OF the trades council,
while congratulating themselves upon
the present standing of the body. crit
tcize the lack of interest shown by
some of the unions.
